title: Array, string, and filesystem unit tests
description:
Add unit tests for the new array, string, and filesystem expansion functions.

Array tests (see spec section 9.3):
- array_sum([3,1,4,1,5])=14(Int), array_sum([])=0, array_sum with floats
- array_min/max with values, error on empty
- array_any/array_all with predicates (requires setting up CallbackInvoker or testing without it using direct function calls)
- array_take/drop with normal, oversized, and zero counts
- array_chunk: normal case, empty array, error on size<=0
- array_unique preserves order, removes duplicates
- array_join with separator, empty array returns ""
- array_slice with positive, negative indices, clamping
- array_find: found and not-found cases

String tests (see spec section 9.4):
- string_index_of: found, not found, empty needle
- string_repeat: normal, zero count, negative errors
- string_pad_left/right: padding needed, already wide enough
- string_bytes("ABC")=[65,66,67]

Filesystem tests (see spec section 9.6):
- file_exists("/tmp")=true, file_exists("/nonexistent")=false
- dir_create + dir_list on temp directory
- path_join, path_dirname, path_basename, path_extension
- file_size with temp file

Note: array_any/all/find tests need CallbackInvoker. If testing at stdlib level without VM, you may need to set up a mock CallbackInvoker or test these only via integration tests.
